title: Inventory mangrove-extent, storm-surge, coastal population, and aquaculture data sources for Southeast Asia
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates mangrove-extent products (GMW), storm-surge models, coastal population data, and aquaculture-expansion data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit country-level coverage assessment.
inputs: Global Mangrove Watch documentation and validation studies
Storm-surge model documentation for Southeast Asian coastlines
Aquaculture production and expansion data from FAO and national sources
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, spatial resolution, temporal coverage, and documented limitations — particularly GMW resolution for narrow fringing mangroves and storm-surge model bathymetry requirements.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, coverage, temporal frequency, and driver-attribution limitations.

title: Map mangrove deforestation trends and drivers at sub-national scale for at least two Southeast Asian countries
outcome: A sub-national mangrove-change analysis for at least two countries, documenting deforestation rates, conversion drivers (aquaculture, agriculture, development), and remaining-mangrove threat classification, with explicit GMW resolution and pre-1996 baseline limitations.
inputs: GMW mangrove-extent time series for target countries
Land-use classification data for driver attribution
Administrative boundary data for sub-national aggregation
Published driver-classification studies for target countries
expected_artifact: validation.md update with deforestation methodology, driver classification, and accuracy assessment.
validation_method: Replicator confirms GMW data extraction, deforestation-rate calculation, driver-classification methodology, and accuracy assessment, with explicit documentation of resolution limitations for narrow fringing mangroves.
done_condition: Deforestation analysis includes methodology, sub-national rates, driver classification, accuracy assessment, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Estimate coastal-protection service loss from mangrove deforestation using storm-surge attenuation modeling
outcome: A coastal-protection loss analysis linking mangrove deforestation to reduced storm-surge attenuation for at least two countries, with explicit attenuation-relationship methodology, bathymetry-data quality documentation, and population-at-risk assessment.
inputs: storm-surge model documentation and Southeast Asian bathymetry data
published attenuation field studies for target coastlines
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with protection-loss methodology and sub-national valuation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms protection-loss methodology, attenuation-relationship application, and population-at-risk assessment, with explicit documentation of bathymetry data quality and model uncertainty.
done_condition: Protection-loss analysis includes methodology, attenuation-relationship documentation, bathymetry-data quality assessment, population-at-risk estimates, and replication status.

title: Red-team protection-valuation overstatement and displacement risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how protection-loss valuations could overstate benefits by ignoring economic drivers of mangrove conversion, how results could be used to displace coastal communities without providing alternatives, and how restoration-potential claims could create false security.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every protection-loss claim has attenuation-relationship limitations, bathymetry-data caveats, and economic-driver documentation. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every protection-loss claim is either blocked, softened to methodology-dependent language with explicit uncertainty bounds, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match coastal-zone management and restoration decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the protection-loss analysis would change any real decision for coastal-zone management authorities and mangrove-restoration practitioners without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for coastal-zone management and restoration audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that protection valuations ignore aquaculture livelihoods or that restoration-potential claims delay protective action. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Indonesian Ministry of Marine Affairs coastal-zone officer), named decision (e.g., mangrove restoration site prioritization), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for aquaculture-ban justification without livelihood alternatives), and replicated field-reality review.
